dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4627 IS: Fostering Agricultural Research and Modernization through Artificial Intelligence Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Budd (for himself, Mr. Schiff, Mr. Banks, Ms. Cortez Masto, Mr. Rounds, and Ms. Blunt Rochester) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry

form.official-title: To increase access to artificial intelligence through programs of the Department of Agriculture, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Fostering Agricultural Research and Modernization through Artificial Intelligence Act of 2026 or the FARM AI Act of 2026.

paragraph.text: artificial intelligence has the potential—

subparagraph.text: to improve farm productivity and security;
to enhance decision-making in crop and animal systems management;
to develop precision technologies for the agriculture industry;
to reduce resource use;
to expand economic opportunities for small, medium, and family-run farms; and
to allow United States farmers to outcompete international competitors;

paragraph.text: rural communities, agribusinesses, farmers, and the food supply chain benefit from technical assistance, education, and outreach to adopt artificial intelligence technologies; and
Federal guidance and coordination are needed to accelerate the responsible adoption of artificial intelligence in agriculture.

subsection.text: It is the sense of Congress that, to support technology development, adoption, and workforce training, the Secretary of Agriculture should increase access to artificial intelligence through—

paragraph.text: the programs and outreach efforts of the Department of Agriculture; and
collaboration with the National Institute of Standards and Technology.

section.header: Inclusion of artificial intelligence as priority area in Department of Agriculture research and extension programs

subsection.text: Subsection (b)(2) of the Competitive, Special, and Facilities Research Grant Act (7 U.S.C. 3157(b)(2)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (E)—

subparagraph.text: in clause (iv), by striking and at the end;
in clause (v), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: research, development, and implementation of artificial intelligence to enhance precision agriculture, farm management, crop production, resource management, cybersecurity, and weather monitoring.

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: Workforce development and technical training, including—

clause.text: implementation of computing systems and artificial intelligence;
precision agriculture adoption and usage; and

subsection.text: Section 1473H(b) of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 (7 U.S.C. 3319k(b)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (2)—

subparagraph.text: in subparagraph (B)—

clause.text: by inserting and deployment after development; and
by inserting artificial intelligence systems, after research tools,; and

subparagraph.text: in subparagraph (C), by inserting artificial intelligence systems, after research tools,; and

paragraph.text: in paragraph (5)—

subparagraph.text: in subparagraph (A), by striking or at the end;
in subparagraph (B), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; or; and
by adding at the end the following:
develop artificial intelligence solutions for farmers or ranchers.

subsection.text: Section 2 of the Act of May 18, 1914 (38 Stat. 373, chapter 79; 7 U.S.C. 342), is amended by inserting including artificial intelligence systems and data-driven agricultural management tools, and providing outreach and education to producers and rural communities on the adoption and use of such technologies, after practices or technologies in agriculture,.

subsection.header: Grants and fellowships for food and agricultural sciences education
subsection.text: Section 1417 of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 (7 U.S.C. 3152) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (b)—

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (5), by striking and at the end;
in paragraph (6), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: to design and implement workforce development and technical training programs for rural communities to teach—

subparagraph.text: the implementation of computing systems and artificial intelligence;
precision agriculture adoption and usage; and
machine maintenance.

paragraph.text: in subsection (c)—

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (1), by striking and at the end;
in paragraph (2), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: applications for teaching enhancement projects that focus on workforce development and technical assistance education programs to develop and implement artificial intelligence systems and precision agriculture technologies.

subsection.text: Subtitle C of the Department of Agriculture Reorganization Act of 1994 (7 U.S.C. 6941 et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: The term Advisor means the Artificial Intelligence Agriculture Advisor described in subsection (b).
The term State means—

subparagraph.text: each of the several States of the United States;
the District of Columbia; and
any territory of the United States.

subsection.text: The Secretary shall designate a senior official from the National Institute of Food and Agriculture to serve as the Artificial Intelligence Agriculture Advisor.

paragraph.text: encourage and promote, in each State, the adoption and use of artificial intelligence and digital agriculture technologies, including—

subparagraph.text: technical assistance and outreach;
workforce training and education;
adoption of precision agriculture tools;
data-driven farm management practices;
animal system management practices;
support for small farm enterprises in using artificial intelligence; and
any other activity relating to artificial intelligence in agriculture, as determined by the Secretary; and

paragraph.text: in collaboration with the National Institute of Standards and Technology, develop and implement national artificial intelligence standards for the agriculture sector.

subsection.text: In carrying out subsection (c), the Advisor shall—

paragraph.text: coordinate with the agencies and officials of the Department;
advise the Secretary on issues relating to artificial intelligence in agriculture;
ensure that the programs of the Department are updated to address best practices in artificial intelligence adoption;
conduct outreach to stakeholders and coordinate external partnerships to share best practices and offer technical assistance to agricultural producers;
facilitate interagency program coordination and develop tools for promoting artificial intelligence adoption;
coordinate networks of agricultural producers using artificial intelligence; and
collaborate with the National Institute of Standards and Technology and other Federal agencies, as needed.

subsection.text: Section 296(b) of the Department of Agriculture Reorganization Act of 1994 (7 U.S.C. 7014(b)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: The authority of the Secretary to carry out section 237.
